rulings.Case Title: RAUL VASQUEZ VS. ROSA SOTO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Default And Default Judgment And For Restoration Of Defendants To Possession
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 3. DEFENDANTS EVANGELINA SOTO, DAVID SOTO and VICENTE SOTO's Motion To Set Aside And Vacate Default And Default Judgment And For Restoration Of Defendants To Possession DENIED. The Sheriff evicted defendants on June 7. A registered process server has declared under penalty of perjury that he attempted to personally serve defendants on six occasions; on two of those occasions, the process server heard noise inside, but occupants would not answer the door. Upon court order, defendants were then served process by mail and by posting. As to the posting, defendants' only response is bald assertions that they were not served; this does not suffice to rebut the presumption of Evidence Code 647. See Palm Property Investments, LLC v. Yadegar (2011) 194 Cal. App. 4th 1419, 1426-28. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JOAN T PHILLIPS VS. DIOGENIES BARELA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 4. DEFENDANTS DIOGENES VARELA and LEYDIS MEDRANO's MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT GRANTED. Defendants shall file answer no later than June 21, 2016 by 4:00 p.m. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: MATHIEU VERBEECK VS. MICHELLE MALLIETT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Michelle Malliet'S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Plainitff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 11. Defendant Michelle Malliet'S Demurrer To Plaintiff's Complaint OVERRULED. Contrary to defendant's assertion, the complaint does not just allege that "she lives in an illegal unit"; it also alleges, inter alia, her dangerous use of appliances.= (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA M. ELSDON VS. RODERIC CRANSTON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT RODERIC CRANSTON's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The complaint adequately alleges substantial interference. The then-applicable "Notice to Tenant" was attached to the notice to vacate. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE CHAN VS. TRUNG KIEN THI ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 7. DEFENDANT TRUNG THAI's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The complaint adequately pleads a cause of action; the demurrer raises factual disputes. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK CHAN VS. PARKE SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default, Vacate Default Judgment, And Recall Writ Of Possession; Mpa
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 8. DEFENDANT PARKE SMITH's Motion To Vacate Entry Of Default, Vacate Default Judgment, And Recall Writ Of Possession DENIED. Two registered process servers declare under penalty of perjury that they served papers on defendant on April 19 and May 17, 2016. Defendant's response is to boldly assert that he was not served on those dates. This does not suffice to rebut the presumption of Evidence Code 647. See Palm Property Investments, LLC v. Yadegar (2011) 194 Cal. App. 4th 1419, 1426-28. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BRENT MARSH VS. ELLEN JAMES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 9. DEFENDANT ELLEN JAMES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED. The complaint adequately pleads unlawful detainer. The parties' prior case has been dismissed. = (501/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: HERBERT ARCEO VS. ARLENE N GLOVER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, June 21, 2016, line 10. PLAINTIFF HERBERT ARCEO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, And For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide complete responses to special interrogatories 1-4, 8, 13, 17-19 by hand delivery within 2 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $810. = (501/RBU)
